title: Demo script: slash-command flow
goal: Rewrite demo/cycle.sh to portray the interactive /mini:* slash-command dialog (a scripted You:/Claude: exchange per step, clearly labelled illustrative) instead of the CLI cycle, kept offline and deterministic; re-rendering cycle.gif via record.sh stays a manual step the user runs.

steps.title: Add You/Claude dialog helpers
steps.detail: Add helpers to cycle.sh that render a chat exchange (you "/mini:next …" / claude "…") with a typewriter effect and visually distinct prefixes, plus one intro disclaimer line marking the Claude prose as illustrative.

steps.title: Rewrite cycle as slash-command exchange
steps.detail: Rewrite each step (init → next → plan → do → done) as a You: types /mini:* + Claude: short reply, while the real mini … --apply runs under the hood so the genuine [ok] output is preserved.

steps.title: Update record.sh + script header
steps.detail: Align the comments/title in record.sh and the cycle.sh header with the new slash-flow content (no mention of a CLI cycle); the asciinema→agg render mechanics stay unchanged.

steps.title: Fix README GIF caption
steps.detail: Rewrite the <sub> caption under the GIF in README so it reflects the slash-command flow instead of equivalent terminal CLI cycle, keeping the link to record.sh.

steps.title: Run cycle.sh offline & verify
steps.detail: Run bash demo/cycle.sh, confirm it completes offline with no live API, shows the dialog plus real [ok] output, and exits cleanly.

resolvedVerify: Re-render demo/cycle.gif from the new script
